goals.short_name: Safe and reliable electricity for Calgary

main_services.name: Have your home connected to and served by Calgary's electrical grid
Have your business connected to and served by Calgary's electrical grid
Be protected from electrical hazards in your community
Choose how you buy and pay for electricity
Receive clear and consolidated billing for your services
Stay connected to electricity when facing financial hardship
Generate and export your own solar energy
Charge electric vehicles at home and at work

acts.summary: Alberta's primary electricity legislation. Establishes the structure of the deregulated electricity market, defines the roles of distribution system owners (like ENMAX Power), retailers, and the regulated rate provider. Governs distribution obligations, customer connection rights, metering, the regulated rate option, competitive retail, micro-generation, and consumer protections.
acts.full_text: Part 1 – Interpretation and Application Part 2 – Purposes of Electricity Legislation, Powers, Duties and Functions Part 3 – Independent System Operator Part 4 – Balancing Pool Part 5 – Liability Part 6 – Generation Part 7 – Distribution 100 Definitions 101 Duties of ISO re distribution 102 Terms and conditions of service 103 Regulated rate option 104 Financial reporting 105 Duties of owner of electric distribution system 106 Code of conduct 107 Regulated rate option provider 108 Regulations Part 8 – Retail 109 Interpretation 110 Right of customers to choose retailer 111 Functions of retailer 112 Default supply 113 Code of conduct 114 Regulations Part 9 – Regulation by the Commission Division 1 – Rate Regulation Division 2 – Facilities Division 3 – Market Rules Division 4 – Compliance and Enforcement Division 5 – Other

sections.title: Distribution — Obligation to Provide Service
sections.description: Section 105 establishes the duties of owners of electric distribution systems, including the obligation to provide service to customers within the service area. Section 101 sets out the ISO's duties regarding distribution. Together they govern ENMAX Power's core obligation to deliver safe, reliable electricity distribution.

services.name: Receive reliable electricity at your home
services.ms_name: Have your home connected to and served by Calgary's electrical grid

services.name: Receive reliable electricity at your business
services.ms_name: Have your business connected to and served by Calgary's electrical grid

sections.title: Distribution — Connection and Extension
sections.description: Section 105 establishes the owner's duties to connect customers and extend the distribution system. Section 102 governs the terms and conditions of service. Section 108 enables regulations governing connection standards, extension policies, and customer contribution requirements.

services.name: Get your home's electrical service modified or upgraded
services.ms_name: Have your home connected to and served by Calgary's electrical grid

services.name: Get electrical infrastructure built for new development
services.ms_name: Have your business connected to and served by Calgary's electrical grid

services.name: Get your business property's electrical service modified or upgraded
services.ms_name: Have your business connected to and served by Calgary's electrical grid

sections.title: Distribution — Third-party Access
sections.description: Section 105 governs the duties of distribution system owners, including terms under which third parties (such as telecommunications providers) may access distribution infrastructure — poles, ducts, and rights-of-way.

services.name: Access utility infrastructure for telecom installations
services.ms_name: Have your business connected to and served by Calgary's electrical grid

sections.title: Distribution — Metering
sections.description: Section 105 establishes the owner's duties regarding metering on the distribution system. Section 108 enables regulations governing meter installation, ownership, testing, and accuracy requirements, ensuring customers can trust that their consumption is measured correctly.

services.name: Have your electricity consumption measured accurately
services.ms_name: Have your home connected to and served by Calgary's electrical grid

sections.title: Regulated Rate Option
sections.description: Section 103 establishes the regulated rate option (RRO) as the default electricity supply for customers who do not choose a competitive retailer. Section 107 designates the regulated rate option provider. Together they define how the RRO is offered and set consumer protections for customers on the regulated rate.

services.name: Choose an electricity rate and plan that fits your budget
services.ms_name: Choose how you buy and pay for electricity

sections.title: Customer Choice and Retail
sections.description: Section 110 establishes the right of customers to choose their electricity retailer. Section 111 defines the functions of retailers. Section 112 governs default supply arrangements. Together they create the competitive retail framework in which ENMAX Energy operates.

services.name: Choose an electricity rate and plan that fits your budget
services.ms_name: Choose how you buy and pay for electricity

services.name: Choose energy supply options that fit your business operations
services.ms_name: Choose how you buy and pay for electricity

sections.title: Disconnection and Reconnection
sections.description: Section 105 establishes the owner's duties regarding service continuity. Section 108 enables regulations governing disconnection, including notice requirements, prohibitions on winter disconnection, reconnection procedures, and hardship protections.

services.name: Get help staying connected when you can't afford your electricity
services.ms_name: Stay connected to electricity when facing financial hardship

sections.title: Micro-generation — Enabling Framework
sections.description: Section 105 establishes distribution system owner duties including accommodating distributed generation connections. Section 108 enables the Micro-generation Regulation (Alta Reg 27/2008), which provides the detailed framework for customers to generate electricity from renewable sources and receive credit for exported power. (Note: Part 5 of the EUA is 'Liability', not micro-generation.)

services.name: Connect your home solar system to the grid and earn credits for exported power
services.ms_name: Generate and export your own solar energy

services.name: Connect your commercial solar or distributed energy system to the grid
services.ms_name: Generate and export your own solar energy

sections.title: Market Structure and Administration
sections.description: Establishes the structure of Alberta's deregulated electricity market, including the separation of generation, transmission, distribution, and retail functions. Governs the Alberta Electric System Operator (AESO) and the framework for market operations.

services.name: Access Calgary's electricity market infrastructure

sections.title: Regulations — Regulatory Framework
sections.description: Authorizes regulations governing tariffs, distribution standards, retail market rules, customer service standards, and reporting obligations. Provides the basis for the detailed regulatory instruments (RRO Regulation, Code of Conduct Regulation, etc.) that govern ENMAX's operations.

services.name: Access regulatory documentation, tariffs, and technical standards

acts.summary: Governs the generation and transmission of electric energy in Alberta. Requires approvals for the construction and operation of power plants and transmission lines. Ensures the adequacy and reliability of Alberta's electricity supply through generation and transmission planning.
acts.full_text:
Part 1 – Interpretation
Part 2 – Generation of Electric Energy
Part 3 – Transmission of Electric Energy
Part 4 – Small Power Research and Development
Part 5 – General Provisions

sections.title: Generation and Transmission Approvals
sections.description: Requires approvals from the AUC for the construction and operation of power plants and transmission facilities. Ensures that generation capacity and transmission infrastructure are adequate to deliver reliable electricity to distribution systems like ENMAX Power's Calgary grid.

services.ms_name: Have your business connected to and served by Calgary's electrical grid

acts.summary: Establishes the Alberta Utilities Commission (AUC) as the regulator for Alberta's electricity and natural gas sectors. The AUC approves rates and tariffs, adjudicates complaints, and oversees market participants including ENMAX Power (distribution) and ENMAX Energy (retail and regulated rate provision).

Part 1 – Interpretation
Part 2 – Alberta Utilities Commission
  Division 1 – Establishment
  Division 2 – Jurisdiction and Powers
  Division 3 – Proceedings
Part 3 – Rates and Tariffs
Part 4 – Market Surveillance Administrator
Part 5 – Appeals
Part 6 – General

sections.title: AUC Jurisdiction — Rate and Tariff Approval
sections.description: Grants the AUC jurisdiction to approve or deny rates and tariffs charged by utilities, including ENMAX Power's distribution tariff and ENMAX Energy's regulated rate. Ensures rates are just, reasonable, and not unduly discriminatory. Governs the process by which tariff schedules and technical standards are approved and published.

services.name: Access regulatory documentation, tariffs, and technical standards

sections.title: Market Surveillance Administrator
sections.description: Continues the Market Surveillance Administrator (MSA) as an independent body to monitor the fair, efficient, and openly competitive operation of Alberta's electricity market. Relevant to ENMAX's compliance with market conduct rules and the competitive separation between ENMAX Power and ENMAX Energy.

services.name: Access Calgary's electricity market infrastructure

acts.summary: Specifies how the regulated rate option (RRO) is calculated for residential and small commercial customers who have not chosen a competitive retailer. ENMAX Energy is the regulated rate provider for ENMAX Power's distribution service area. The regulation sets the methodology for rate calculation, hedging requirements, and consumer protections.
acts.full_text:
Part 1 – Interpretation
Part 2 – Regulated Rate Option
Part 3 – Rate Calculation
Part 4 – Consumer Protections
Part 5 – Reporting

sections.title: Regulated Rate Calculation and Application
sections.description: Defines who is eligible for the regulated rate option (customers who have not chosen a competitive retailer), how the rate is calculated based on market prices with hedging, and the obligations of the regulated rate provider (ENMAX Energy) in setting and communicating the rate.

services.name: Choose an electricity rate and plan that fits your budget
services.ms_name: Choose how you buy and pay for electricity

acts.summary: Regulates the conduct of distribution system owners (ENMAX Power) and their affiliated retailers (ENMAX Energy) to ensure fair competition in the retail electricity market. Requires operational and information separation, protects customer information confidentiality, ensures equal access to distribution services for all retailers, and prevents cross-subsidization.
acts.full_text:
Part 1 – Interpretation
Part 2 – Objectives
Part 3 – Conduct of Owners
Part 4 – Conduct of Affiliated Retailers
Part 5 – Customer Information
Part 6 – Compliance Plans
Part 7 – Complaints

sections.title: Separation of Distribution and Retail Operations
sections.description: Requires that ENMAX Power (distribution) and ENMAX Energy (retail) operate with appropriate separation to prevent the distribution monopoly from giving the affiliated retailer an unfair advantage. Governs information barriers, operational separation, and equal treatment of all retailers accessing the distribution system.

services.name: Access Calgary's electricity market infrastructure

sections.title: Customer Information Protection
sections.description: Protects the confidentiality of customer information collected through distribution services. Prohibits ENMAX Power from sharing customer information with ENMAX Energy or any other retailer without customer consent, ensuring fair competition and customer privacy.

services.name: Access regulatory documentation, tariffs, and technical standards

acts.summary: Governs the interconnection of small-scale generating units (under 5 MW) to the distribution system. Enables residential and commercial customers to generate electricity from solar and other renewable sources, connect to the grid through ENMAX Power, and receive credit for energy exported to the distribution system.
acts.full_text:
Part 1 – Interpretation
Part 2 – Micro-generation
  Division 1 – Application and Connection
  Division 2 – Metering and Settlement
  Division 3 – Credit for Exported Electricity

sections.title: Micro-generation Interconnection and Credit
sections.description: Establishes the process for customers to apply to connect micro-generation systems (such as rooftop solar panels) to the distribution system. Defines metering requirements, how exported electricity is measured, and the credit mechanism by which customers offset their consumption with their generation. ENMAX Power processes interconnection applications and manages the billing credits.

acts.summary: Establishes the framework for workplace health and safety in Alberta. Relevant to ENMAX's operations in maintaining electrical infrastructure and to public safety around power lines, substations, and other electrical facilities. Defines duties of employers, workers, and the public regarding electrical hazards.
acts.full_text:
Part 1 – Purpose, Application and Interpretation
Part 2 – Duties
Part 3 – Occupational Health and Safety Code
Part 4 – Officers
Part 5 – Administrative Penalties
Part 6 – Offences and Penalties
Part 7 – General

sections.title: Duties Regarding Electrical Hazards
sections.description: Establishes duties of employers, workers, and contractors to ensure safety around electrical infrastructure. The OHS Code (enabled under Part 3) sets specific technical rules for work near power lines, minimum approach distances, and requirements for electrical safety training. Relevant to both ENMAX workers and third parties working near ENMAX infrastructure.

services.name: Live and work safely around electrical infrastructure in your community

services.name: Access safety standards and training for working near electrical infrastructure
services.ms_name: Be protected from electrical hazards in your community

acts.summary: Establishes Alberta's safety codes system, which sets technical standards for buildings, electrical systems, plumbing, gas, and other safety disciplines. Relevant to ENMAX services involving electrical permits, inspections, and compliance with the Alberta Electrical Code for service connections, modifications, and installations.

sections.title: Electrical Permits, Inspections, and Safety Codes
sections.description: Governs the permit and inspection process for electrical work, including service connections, modifications, and upgrades. The Alberta Electrical Code (adopted under this Act) sets the technical standards that electrical installations must meet. Relevant when customers modify their electrical service or install new systems (such as EV charging or solar panels) that require electrical permits.

services.name: Access safety standards and training for working near electrical infrastructure
services.ms_name: Be protected from electrical hazards in your community
